preamble: The backing and lenition of the dental stop [d] into the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is a less common transformation, typically found in specific phonetic environments or as part of larger consonant shifts.

examples.note: While [d] became the fricative [ð], further shifts in articulation can lead to backed fricatives in certain dialects.
Backing can occur in the context of backing vowel shifts or as a result of systemic lenition where the place of articulation retreats.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
